element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Stuns basic enemies; later upgraded to platform creation
Obtained through NPC interaction; third weapon type
Grants temporary invincibility and auto-run 'quack attack' mode
Enables wall-climbing and traversal of longer distances; requires precise timing
Gated progression; bosses resistant to plunger stuns but vulnerable to upgraded ammo

typical_relief_triggers: Completing overland section and calling nephews' biplane to exit
Finding clue items that solve upcoming puzzles
typical_tension_triggers: Entering dungeon sections with locked progression gates
Puzzle-gating that requires backtracking for clues
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Methodical exploration-based platformer with puzzle-driven gating; slower pace than pure action games

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Overland sections can be explored in player-chosen order; not all dungeons accessible until specific prerequisites met

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle introduction to platforming and basic combat; safe contexts for learning plunger mechanics
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Introduction of puzzle-gating and upgraded abilities; moderate difficulty in traversal
difficulty_curve.late_game: Boss encounters and complex plunger-platform sequences; highest skill requirements
notable_spikes: First boss encounter after Transylvania (Count Dracula's castle)
Viking ship section with undead enemies
Final treasure location requiring mastery of plunger-as-platform mechanic

notable_level_patterns.description: Each major world location divided into two sections: open overland area (explorable, with clue-gathering) and dungeon (puzzle-locked, progression-gated). Player may choose overland exploration order but dungeon access is sequenced.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Mexico, Transylvania, India, Egypt, Viking Ship, South Pole, final King Garuzia temple

notable_level_patterns.description: Vital clues discovered in specific overland sections are required to solve puzzles in later dungeons. Creates soft-gating through information rather than hard locks.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Complete map obtained from Transylvania; diary location revealed at South Pole needed for final treasure

notable_level_patterns.description: Plunger gun mechanics upgrade mid-game to enable new movement types (wall-climbing via platform creation, bird-riding traversal). Later level design requires these upgraded abilities.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Levels requiring bird-based traversal and wall-climbing sections
